scorecard.Why it matters: Measures real access, not activity volume
Creates enough shots in a low-volume model
Turns known relationships into faster-cycle pipeline
Keeps proposals tied to quality opportunities

tier1.title: Protect & Expand
tier1.heading: Engagement priorities
tier1.points:
Deepen enterprise partnership across multiple functions and buyer levels within the account
Identify and pursue adjacent opportunities — new brands, therapeutic areas, or decision cycles
Deliver Ring Session-quality outputs consistently to reinforce defensible, decision-ready value
Establish a formal QBR cadence to entrench LCN as the intelligence system of record
tier1.goal: Year-end goal: 3 accounts broadened by year-end

tier2.title: Reactivate & Re-Qualify
tier2.heading: Reactivation approach
tier2.points:
Re-engage through value-forward outreach — lead with an insight or market development, not a pitch
Revisit the original pain point: has the business decision environment shifted since last engagement?
Leverage Dimensional Intelligence proof points and the SHU validation to reset credibility
Map to the right current buyer — contacts shift; align to today's decision-maker, not yesterday's
tier2.goal: 6-month goal: 3-4 reactivated into live dialogue by 6 months

tier3.title: Qualify & Convert
tier3.heading: Net-new acquisition strategy
tier3.points:
Target look-alike profiles to best existing accounts — same buyer persona, analogous therapeutic context
Enter with a pre-emptive proposal anchored to a validated business need, not a capabilities overview
Use thought leadership and Atomic Essays to build familiarity before the first direct outreach
Qualify rigorously before committing founder time — CRM must show stage, owner, and next step
tier3.goal: Full-year goal: 1-2 net-new wins in 0-6 months · 4-6 across full year
